source_jobs.title: Sr. Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At Tapcart, we believe every brand needs a direct relationship with their customers - one they actually own.

We are the mobile commerce engine that powers shopping for Shopify's fastest-growing brands. $15B+ in revenue processed. 200M+ shoppers worldwide. Brands like Princess Polly, Béis, Forever 21, and Young LA rely on us to power a large portion of their revenue.

We're a lean team with minimal management and big autonomy. Everyone here builds and code is core to what we do - engineers, product, leadership. We use our own platform, we talk to merchants daily, and we ship constantly. Backed by Shopify, Silver Lake and other top VCs. Profitable and growing

If you want a place where you own real outcomes, not just tickets, you'll fit in.




WHY YOU SHOULD JOIN TAPCART, WHEN YOU HAVE A SEA OF OTHER OPTIONS?

 - You want to work with colleagues who have high standards and who love building beautiful, performant software

 - You prefer the dopamine of working on software that will get into hundreds of millions of users' hands, vs working on enterprise, forward-deployed software




WE DEEPLY CARE ABOUT USING AI IN OUR WORK. DON'T PROCEED WITH APPLYING IF:

 - You don't love agents / don't use them daily

 - You don't value craft and cannot speak to software that inspires you




WHAT YOU’LL BE WORKING ON AT TAPCART

 - Architect and ship core product that feels durable, fast and clean. Work across multiple stacks to deliver impact.

 - Design our proactive AI agent orchestration layers used by thousands of high growth brands

 - Prototype new interaction patterns for human-agent collaboration

 - Raise our bar for engineering craft, help with recruiting, and hold the line on quality

 - Dogfood the platform and our apps every day :)

You'll be a true owner, from prototype to production, and work side‑by‑side with the CEO, design, and key stakeholders, all while staying close to merchants to deliver value early and iterate quickly.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of engineering and product work

 - Demonstrated skills across frontend, backend and database layers

 - A Github that is more green than black




SALARY RANGE

$175,000 - $350,000 - we pay for exceptional




BENEFITS

Competitive salary and equity. Medical, dental, vision. 401(k). Flexible PTO. Remote-friendly. Home office stipend. Quarterly merchant stipend. Lots of swag drops

source_jobs.title: Agency Partner Manager
source_jobs.description:
Tapcart is the leading mobile app platform for the world’s fastest-growing Shopify brands. We help marketers and eCommerce teams strengthen their brands and create differentiated customer experiences through a direct mobile channel.

Our platform powers mobile shopping for over 50 million consumers and processes more than $8 billion in revenue annually. Brands like Béis, Grove Collaborative, and Ruggable trust Tapcart to turn mobile traffic into high-retention, high-growth relationships — with mobile apps launched in hours, not months.



THE ROLE

Tapcart’s agency partners aren’t just referral sources – they’re building service lines on our platform, co-selling with our team, and shaping how the mobile app category is adopted across the Shopify landscape.

That’s why we’re seeking Agency Partner Managers who are product-savvy, technically curious, and commercially accountable. This is a hands-on operator role – for someone who wants to teach, lead, and drive execution with top-tier agencies.



HOW YOU WILL MAKE AN IMPACT

 - Get deep into the Tapcart product, fast – and use that fluency to guide partners toward the right messaging, skills development, and service opportunities.

 - Prescribe, not react – giving partners a clear, strategic path to success based on what’s in demand and what’s working across the ecosystem.

 - Multi-thread within each agency – building relationships with leadership, sales, delivery, and marketing to drive full-funnel partner engagement.

 - Collaborate with partners on how to monetize services built around Tapcart, including implementation, retention strategy, and mobile app best practices.

 - Drive thoughtful, well-planned execution — including account mapping, co-selling motions, GTM campaigns, and partner-led activations.

WHAT SKILLS YOU’LL NEED

 - 5+ years of experience in B2B SaaS, with at least 2 years in partnerships, sales, solutions consulting, or a GTM-facing role requiring product depth.

 - A clear track record of driving partner-led or partner-influenced pipeline through strategic execution.

 - Confidence navigating technical conversations – you’re not a developer, but you’re comfortable explaining product architecture and integrations.

 - Deep familiarity with how Shopify agencies operate, monetize, and scale.

 - You take initiative and move fast, but you stay grounded in the strategy and know when to bring others in.

 - You can hold a room with strong communication and dynamic presentation skills.

BONUS IF YOU HAVE

If some of these describe you, you’ll thrive in this role – but if not, we still want to hear from you.

 - You’ve worked in or closely with Shopify agencies and understand how they think about service, margin, and growth.

 - You have a background in solutions consulting, sales engineering, enablement, or product marketing – and love translating product into action.

 - You’re curious about AI and how it can improve enablement, productivity, or partner execution.

 - You’ve helped partners package and sell services around a product before - and know how to connect value to monetization.

 - You’re excited by the idea of building repeatable programs, scalable GTM plays, and documentation others can run with.

*This role involves moderate travel for field events, partner on-sites, and marketing activations.

This position is remote eligible in the following states: AL, AZ, CA, CO, FL, GA, IL, MA, ME, MI, NV, NJ, NY, NC, OH, OR, PA, TN, TX, UT, VA, WA

We are ideally looking for candidates based in the LA or NYC area.


SALARY RANGE: Salary range: $130,000-$140,000 base + commission (commensurate with experience)


TAPCART PERKS AND BENEFITS:

 - 💰 Financial Wellness
   
   - 401(k) plan and equity grants for all full-time employees — because we grow together
   
   - Competitive compensation with room to grow

 - 🏥 Health & Wellbeing
   
   - Affordable, high-quality medical, dental, and vision coverage
   
   - Mental health support via Headspace and Talkspace

source_jobs.title: Account Executive (NY/LA)
source_jobs.description:
At Tapcart, we believe every brand needs a direct relationship with their customers - one they actually own.

We are the mobile commerce engine that powers shopping for Shopify's fastest-growing brands. $15B+ in revenue processed. 200M+ shoppers worldwide. Brands like Princess Polly, Béis, Forever 21, and Young LA rely on us to power a large portion of their revenue.

We're a lean team with minimal management and big autonomy. Everyone here builds and code is core to what we do - engineers, product, leadership. We use our own platform, we talk to merchants daily, and we ship constantly. Backed by Shopify, Silver Lake and other top VCs. Profitable and growing

If you want a place where you own real outcomes, not just tickets, you'll fit in.



Why join Tapcart when you have a sea of other options?

 - You love the brands you shop from and want them as prospects

 - You want to sell a product that actually works and is a “need-to-have”

 - You'll have a manager who clears the path, not one who watches every step



Don’t proceed with applying if:

 - You don’t use AI tools daily to work faster and smarter

 - Ecommerce is new territory for you. Prospects expect you to understand their business

 - You buy your pants from amazon and dont support DTC



What you’ll be working on at Tapcart

 - Get in front of the brands building the future of DTC and help them see what a great mobile experience can do for their business

 - Use AI to research accounts, personalize outreach, and create assets that deliver value to your prospect’s inbox

 - Bring energy into every call with merchants and teammates alike



You’ll be a true owner from first email to signed contract and work side-by-side with Sales leadership, Solutions, and key stakeholders, while staying close to the customer to deliver value early and iterate quickly.



Qualifications

 - 3+ years of closing experience in SaaS ecommerce or Shopify ecosystem is required, not a nice-to-have

 - A track record you can point to quota attainment, deal size, what you learned when you missed

 - Comfortable prospecting. You’re supported by other channels, but this isn’t a pure inbound role.

 - You don’t sleep on leads. Ever. You’ll get on a plane if that’s what it takes.



Salary + Commission

$240,000–$280,000 OTE (50/50)



Benefits

Competitive salary and equity. Medical, dental, vision. 401(k). Flexible PTO. Remote-friendly. Home office stipend. Quarterly merchant stipend. Lots of swag drops.

source_jobs.title: Marketing Ops Manager
source_jobs.description:
Tapcart is the leading mobile app platform for the world's fastest-growing Shopify brands. We help marketers and eCommerce teams strengthen their brands and create differentiated customer experiences through a direct mobile channel.

Our platform powers mobile shopping for over 50 million consumers and processes more than $8 billion in revenue annually. Brands like Béis, Grove Collaborative, and Ruggable trust Tapcart to turn mobile traffic into high-retention, high-growth relationships — with mobile apps launched in hours, not months.



THE ROLE

We're looking for a Marketing Manager to join our team — someone who sits confidently at the intersection of marketing operations, product marketing, and execution. This isn't a pure ops role, and it isn't a pure PMM role. It's both, and that's what makes it interesting.

You'll own the systems and programs that keep our marketing engine running at a high level — HubSpot infrastructure, conversion optimization, customer marketing — while also playing a central role in how we bring our product to market. You'll also serve as the team's operational anchor: keeping campaigns on track, managing the marketing calendar, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks.

The ideal candidate is equally comfortable building a nurture workflow, writing a product one-pager, and running a CRO experiment. You're a structured thinker who doesn't need a playbook handed to you — you build the playbook.



HOW YOU WILL MAKE AN IMPACT

Own marketing operations end-to-end: You'll be the primary owner of our HubSpot instance — maintaining workflows, lead scoring, contact management, segmentation, and reporting. You'll ensure our marketing data is clean, our attribution is accurate, and our funnel metrics tell a true story. You'll partner closely with Rev Ops and Sales Ops to keep our systems aligned.

Drive conversion rate optimization across the funnel: You'll own CRO across our key marketing surfaces — website, landing pages, email, and paid — running structured A/B tests and using data to identify drop-off points and improve them. You approach experimentation methodically and follow insights all the way through to implementation.

Bring product marketing rigor to our GTM motion: You'll support new product launches with messaging frameworks, positioning documents, sales enablement assets, and competitive intelligence. You'll help translate what the product does into language that resonates with buyers — and keep our core marketing assets up to date as the product evolves.

Build and run customer marketing programs: You'll own lifecycle communications for our existing customers — onboarding sequences, expansion campaigns, and retention programs. You'll work with Customer Success to build a customer reference pipeline and find ways to turn happy customers into active Tapcart advocates.

Serve as the team's operational backbone: You'll own the marketing calendar, project tracking, and cross-functional coordination that keeps the team moving. You'll flag blockers, close loops, and make sure campaigns and launches land on time. In a small team, this kind of operational ownership is a force multiplier.

Use AI to accelerate your output: You'll leverage AI tools to move faster — automating repetitive tasks, improving workflow efficiency, and helping the team get more done with less. You're not afraid to build something new if it solves a real problem.



WHAT SKILLS YOU'LL NEED (5+ YEARS OF EXPERIENCE)

Marketing operations depth: You have strong HubSpot proficiency — you can build workflows, pull segmented reports, troubleshoot attribution, and maintain list hygiene without supervision. You understand how the marketing tech stack connects to the broader GTM motion.

Conversion rate optimization experience: You've run CRO experiments — A/B tests, multivariate tests, or iterative page-level optimizations — and know how to read the results and act on them. You approach conversion as a system to be improved, n

source_jobs.title: Demand Gen Director
source_jobs.description:
Tapcart is the leading mobile app platform for the world's fastest-growing Shopify brands. We help marketers and eCommerce teams strengthen their brands and create differentiated customer experiences through a direct mobile channel.

Our platform powers mobile shopping for over 50 million consumers and processes more than $8 billion in revenue annually. Brands like Béis, Aviator Nation, and Ruggable trust Tapcart to turn mobile traffic into high-retention, high-growth relationships — with mobile apps launched in hours, not months.

THE ROLE

We're looking for a Director of Marketing (or Director of Demand Gen, depending on your background) to join our growing team at a pivotal moment in Tapcart's growth. This is a senior role that requires someone who can think at a strategic level and execute at a rapid pace.

You'll be a key partner to the Head of Marketing, owning our demand generation engine while also contributing to broader marketing strategy, GTM planning, and team leadership. This is a classic startup marketing role with a director-level scope: you'll shape how we grow, manage real budget, influence how the team operates, and get your hands dirty doing the work.

The ideal candidate has a strong demand gen and B2B SaaS background, a genuine command of AI-powered marketing systems, and the strategic instincts to evolve our positioning and GTM approach as the business scales.



HOW YOU WILL MAKE AN IMPACT

Own and scale our demand generation engine: You'll take full ownership of multi-channel pipeline generation — paid media, email, events, content, partner channels, SEO/AEO — with accountability for qualified pipeline and ROI. You know how to build a demand program that compounds over time, not just one that spends budget.

Bring strategic marketing leadership to the team: You'll help define Tapcart's marketing strategy at the category level — refining our ICP targeting, messaging, campaign architecture, and go-to-market approach. You'll be someone the Head of Marketing can think out loud with and hand the keys to (and vice versa).

Build and optimize GTM systems: You'll architect and maintain the systems that power our go-to-market motion: HubSpot infrastructure, lead scoring, attribution models, nurture workflows, and signal-to-sequence programs. You build things that work while you sleep.

Use AI to 10x our output: You'll be at the forefront of AI-driven marketing, deploying tools and automations that multiply the team's output — from trigger-based outbound and content systems to workflow automation and performance analysis. You build with AI, not just use it.

Cross-functional collaboration and leadership: You’ll driving alignment with Sales, Rev Ops, Product, and CS and contribute to how we structure and prioritize as the teams grow. You're a player-coach who makes the people around them better.

Connect brand to pipeline: You'll ensure Tapcart's campaigns and content aren't just producing impressions, they're producing pipeline. You'll collaborate on brand positioning and campaign concepts while holding yourself accountable to business outcomes.



WHAT SKILLS YOU'LL NEED

Proven demand generation leadership: You've owned multi-channel demand programs in a B2B SaaS environment and have a track record of building qualified pipeline. You've managed real budgets and can speak the language of marketing and sales metrics/KPIs.

Strategic marketing instincts: You can zoom out and develop a positioning framework or GTM strategy, then zoom in and execute a campaign. You're not purely a performance marketer — you understand brand, narrative, and how they compound with paid and organic channels.

AI-powered operational leverage: You use AI tools to build systems and workflows — automating research, enrichment, content production, and reporting — not just to write copy faster. You can evaluate new tools quickly and deploy them with purpose.

Deep marketing tech fluency: You have strong working knowledge of HubSpot (or a com

source_jobs.title: Technical Support Representative — APAC
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT TAPCART

Tapcart is the leading mobile commerce platform for Shopify brands. We power native iOS and Android apps for thousands of merchants — from emerging DTC labels to global household names — and help them turn their best customers into repeat shoppers. Our team is fully remote across the US and beyond, and we're growing our support footprint to provide true follow-the-sun coverage for our customers.



POSITION SUMMARY

We're hiring our first AU-based Technical Support Representative to be the frontline expert for our merchants during APAC hours. You'll be the first point of contact for inbound and outbound support across email, live chat, and phone, owning issues from triage through resolution.

You'll close the coverage gap during the US team's overnight, hand off cleanly when the US team comes online, and partner closely with Product, Engineering, and Customer Success to advocate for merchants and continuously improve the support experience.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

• Provide responsive, accurate technical support to merchants via email, live chat, and phone during AEDT/AEST business hours, with a planned overlap window with our US team for handoffs.

• Own the support queue end-to-end during APAC hours — triage, troubleshoot, resolve, and follow up.

• Troubleshoot app behavior, Shopify integrations (themes, scopes, checkout), and third-party integrations like Klaviyo, Algolia, Yotpo, and Recharge.

• Investigate issues across the Tapcart stack — including our React Native iOS/Android apps, Toolshed (our internal admin tool), and merchant builds in Bitrise.

• Reproduce issues using device simulators, Charles/Proxyman, browser dev tools, and log readers; capture clean repro steps so engineering can act fast.

• Triage and escalate bugs with well-scoped tickets in Asana as needed.

• Proactively follow up on unresolved issues, communicate product changes that affect merchants, and surface trends to Customer Success and Product.

• Contribute to our Intercom Help Center articles, internal Confluence runbooks, support macros, and Fin-powered AI workflows so we keep raising the floor on self-service.

• Be the merchant's voice internally — share patterns, friction, and feature gaps with Product and Engineering and advocate for prioritization.


COVERAGE & SCHEDULE

This role is built for APAC coverage. You'll work standard AEDT/AEST business hours from Australia with a daily 2–3 hour overlap with our US-based Support team (typically early-morning AU / late-afternoon US) for warm handoffs of in-flight tickets, weekly team syncs, and live coaching.



WHAT YOUR FIRST 60 DAYS LOOK LIKE

Days 1–30 — Learn the stack

• Complete onboarding on Tapcart's product, Toolshed, and our support stack (Intercom + Fin, Asana, Confluence, Slack).

• Shadow US Support and Solutions teammates, ride along on live tickets, and build a mental model of our most common issues.

• Begin owning Tier 1 tickets end-to-end during AEDT business hours.



Days 31–60 — Own the queue

• Run the APAC queue with backup from US Support for complex issues.

• Develop fluency in our integration ecosystem — Shopify, Klaviyo, Algolia, Yotpo, Recharge — and the most common merchant flows.

• Begin contributing to Help Center articles and Fin training data based on the patterns you see.



WHAT YOU BRING

• 3–5 years of technical support experience, ideally in a SaaS or e-commerce environment supporting mid-market or enterprise customers.

• Strong working knowledge of Shopify (themes, admin, APIs) and the mobile app ecosystem (App Store + Google Play submissions, builds, releases).

• Comfort triaging issues across web and native mobile, with hands-on troubleshooting using tools like Proxyman, Xcode/Android simulators, and browser dev tools.

• Proficiency with helpdesk platforms (Intercom, Zendesk, or Gorgias) and ticketing tools like Asana or Jira.

• Excellent written and verbal communication, with the ability to make complex technical issues feel simple to a

source_jobs.title: Sr. AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At Tapcart, we believe every brand needs a direct relationship with their customers - one they actually own.

We are the mobile commerce engine that powers shopping for Shopify's fastest-growing brands. $15B+ in revenue processed. 200M+ shoppers worldwide. Brands like Princess Polly, Béis, Forever 21, and Young LA rely on us to power a large portion of their revenue.

We're a lean team with minimal management and big autonomy. Everyone here builds and code is core to what we do - engineers, product, leadership. We use our own platform, we talk to merchants daily, and we ship constantly. Backed by Shopify, Silver Lake and other top VCs. Profitable and growing

If you want a place where you own real outcomes, not just tickets, you'll fit in.




WHY YOU SHOULD JOIN TAPCART, WHEN YOU HAVE A SEA OF OTHER OPTIONS?

 - You want to work with colleagues who have high standards and who love building beautiful, performant software

 - You prefer the dopamine of working on software that will get into hundreds of millions of users' hands, vs working on enterprise, forward-deployed software




WE DEEPLY CARE ABOUT USING AI IN OUR WORK. DON'T PROCEED WITH APPLYING IF:

 - You don't love agents / don't use them daily

 - You don't value craft and cannot speak to software that inspires you




WHAT YOU’LL BE WORKING ON AT TAPCART

 - Architect and ship core product that feels durable, fast and clean. Work across multiple stacks to deliver impact.

 - Design our proactive AI agent orchestration layers used by thousands of high growth brands

 - Prototype new interaction patterns for human-agent collaboration

 - Raise our bar for engineering craft, help with recruiting, and hold the line on quality

 - Dogfood the platform and our apps every day :)

You'll be a true owner, from prototype to production, and work side‑by‑side with the CEO, design, and key stakeholders, all while staying close to merchants to deliver value early and iterate quickly.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of engineering and product work

 - Demonstrated skills across frontend, backend and database layers

 - A Github that is more green than black




SALARY RANGE

$175,000 - $350,000 - we pay for exceptional




BENEFITS

Competitive salary and equity. Medical, dental, vision. 401(k). Flexible PTO. Remote-friendly. Home office stipend. Quarterly merchant stipend. Lots of swag drops

source_jobs.title: Senior Account Executive (NY/LA)
source_jobs.description:
At Tapcart, we believe every brand needs a direct relationship with their customers - one they actually own.

We are the mobile commerce engine that powers shopping for Shopify's fastest-growing brands. $15B+ in revenue processed. 200M+ shoppers worldwide. Brands like Princess Polly, Béis, Forever 21, and Young LA rely on us to power a large portion of their revenue.

We're a lean team with minimal management and big autonomy. Everyone here builds and code is core to what we do - engineers, product, leadership. We use our own platform, we talk to merchants daily, and we ship constantly. Backed by Shopify, Silver Lake and other top VCs. Profitable and growing

If you want a place where you own real outcomes, not just tickets, you'll fit in.



Why join Tapcart when you have a sea of other options?

 - You love the brands you shop from and want them as prospects

 - You want to sell a product that actually works and is a “need-to-have”

 - You'll have a manager who clears the path, not one who watches every step



Don’t proceed with applying if:

 - You don’t use AI tools daily to work faster and smarter

 - Ecommerce is new territory for you. Prospects expect you to understand their business

 - You buy your pants from amazon and dont support DTC



What you’ll be working on at Tapcart

 - Get in front of the brands building the future of DTC and help them see what a great mobile experience can do for their business

 - Use AI to research accounts, personalize outreach, and create assets that deliver value to your prospect’s inbox

 - Bring energy into every call with merchants and teammates alike



You’ll be a true owner from first email to signed contract and work side-by-side with Sales leadership, Solutions, and key stakeholders, while staying close to the customer to deliver value early and iterate quickly.



Qualifications

 - 3+ years of closing experience in SaaS ecommerce or Shopify ecosystem is required, not a nice-to-have

 - A track record you can point to quota attainment, deal size, what you learned when you missed

 - Comfortable prospecting. You’re supported by other channels, but this isn’t a pure inbound role.

 - You don’t sleep on leads. Ever. You’ll get on a plane if that’s what it takes.



Salary + Commission

$240,000–$280,000 OTE (50/50)



Benefits

Competitive salary and equity. Medical, dental, vision. 401(k). Flexible PTO. Remote-friendly. Home office stipend. Quarterly merchant stipend. Lots of swag drops.

source_jobs.title: Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
Tapcart is the leading mobile app platform for the world's fastest-growing Shopify brands. We help marketers and eCommerce teams strengthen their brands and create differentiated customer experiences through a direct mobile channel.

Our platform powers mobile shopping for over 50 million consumers and processes more than $8 billion in revenue annually. Brands like Béis, Grove Collaborative, and Ruggable trust Tapcart to turn mobile traffic into high-retention, high-growth relationships — with mobile apps launched in hours, not months.



THE ROLE

We're looking for a Marketing Manager to join our team — someone who sits confidently at the intersection of marketing operations, product marketing, and execution. This isn't a pure ops role, and it isn't a pure PMM role. It's both, and that's what makes it interesting.

You'll own the systems and programs that keep our marketing engine running at a high level — HubSpot infrastructure, conversion optimization, customer marketing — while also playing a central role in how we bring our product to market. You'll also serve as the team's operational anchor: keeping campaigns on track, managing the marketing calendar, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks.

The ideal candidate is equally comfortable building a nurture workflow, writing a product one-pager, and running a CRO experiment. You're a structured thinker who doesn't need a playbook handed to you — you build the playbook.



HOW YOU WILL MAKE AN IMPACT

Own marketing operations end-to-end: You'll be the primary owner of our HubSpot instance — maintaining workflows, lead scoring, contact management, segmentation, and reporting. You'll ensure our marketing data is clean, our attribution is accurate, and our funnel metrics tell a true story. You'll partner closely with Rev Ops and Sales Ops to keep our systems aligned.

Drive conversion rate optimization across the funnel: You'll own CRO across our key marketing surfaces — website, landing pages, email, and paid — running structured A/B tests and using data to identify drop-off points and improve them. You approach experimentation methodically and follow insights all the way through to implementation.

Bring product marketing rigor to our GTM motion: You'll support new product launches with messaging frameworks, positioning documents, sales enablement assets, and competitive intelligence. You'll help translate what the product does into language that resonates with buyers — and keep our core marketing assets up to date as the product evolves.

Build and run customer marketing programs: You'll own lifecycle communications for our existing customers — onboarding sequences, expansion campaigns, and retention programs. You'll work with Customer Success to build a customer reference pipeline and find ways to turn happy customers into active Tapcart advocates.

Serve as the team's operational backbone: You'll own the marketing calendar, project tracking, and cross-functional coordination that keeps the team moving. You'll flag blockers, close loops, and make sure campaigns and launches land on time. In a small team, this kind of operational ownership is a force multiplier.

Use AI to accelerate your output: You'll leverage AI tools to move faster — automating repetitive tasks, improving workflow efficiency, and helping the team get more done with less. You're not afraid to build something new if it solves a real problem.



WHAT SKILLS YOU'LL NEED (5+ YEARS OF EXPERIENCE)

Marketing operations depth: You have strong HubSpot proficiency — you can build workflows, pull segmented reports, troubleshoot attribution, and maintain list hygiene without supervision. You understand how the marketing tech stack connects to the broader GTM motion.

Conversion rate optimization experience: You've run CRO experiments — A/B tests, multivariate tests, or iterative page-level optimizations — and know how to read the results and act on them. You approach conversion as a system to be improved, n

notes: Highly technical job postings, with a focus on mobile commerce and Shopify integrations.
